title: AUTOMATION — Lights-Out Operations
tagline: Your OS is 78% built and 20% used. ~50 min to a machine that works while you sleep.
fin_text: The machine runs without you. Check the board in the morning, not the work.

levels.title: LEVEL 0 · THE GAP
levels.html:
Your audit, one line

You built nucleus, agentbus, agentwatch, and a skills rack — and the 30-day audit shows agentbus: 19 tasks, 1 done, all manual relay; agentwatch: never started once. You own a factory and you're hand-carrying every part between stations.

Why this is an ADHD tax, not a chore backlog

Every manual relay is a context switch, and context switches are the most expensive thing in your day. Automation isn't about saving minutes — it's about protecting the one focus thread you get. The bar for this course: one task auto-closes at 10pm while you sleep.

quiz.q: What's the real cost of manually relaying agent tasks?
opts: The context switch that breaks your focus thread
quiz.ex: Minutes are cheap; refocusing after a switch costs you 15–25 minutes of momentum each time. The machine exists so your brain stays on ONE thing.

mission.txt: Run `bin/agentbus board` (or ask Claude to). Look at the 18 open tasks with fresh eyes — this is your machine, idling.

levels.title: LEVEL 1 · THE BUS — triage the ledger
levels.html:
How the bus is supposed to flow

add → claim → done. A task that sits unclaimed for 10 days isn't a task — it's guilt with a timestamp. Your ledger right now: T001–T005 (SSA launch) open since June 1, T006/T007 (record videos) open 10 days, T015–T019 (weekly research) never claimed.

The triage rule: every open task gets one of three fates

  • KILL — no longer true? Delete without ceremony.
  • DELEGATE — an agent can do it? Route it (next levels make this automatic).
  • GATE — only Zorba can do it? It goes to the Gate Queue (Level 3), never loose on the board.

A clean board is not cosmetic — it's what makes the standup trustworthy.

quiz.q: A task has sat open and unclaimed for 10 days. What is it?
opts: Guilt with a timestamp — triage it: kill, delegate, or gate
quiz.ex: Stale tasks poison the board: once you stop trusting the ledger, you stop using the OS. Triage is maintenance on TRUST, not on tasks.

mission.txt: Triage all 18 open tasks RIGHT NOW with Claude: each one gets kill / delegate / gate. Ten minutes, board clean.

levels.title: LEVEL 2 · DEPLOY AGENTWATCH
levels.html:
The loop you already own

bin/agentwatch watches the bus and auto-runs tasks routed to free local agents (ollama/hermes). Paid APIs and human-judgment work stay manual — that's the designed split. It has never been started. Today it starts.

The deployment checklist

  • Start it (with a log file, so silence is diagnosable).
  • Route ONE low-stakes task to a local agent (e.g. ssa-research: 'list 5 competitor offers').
  • Verify the loop: claim appears → output lands → task closes → handoff in nucleus.

One full cycle proves the machine; scaling is just more routing.

quiz.q: Which work belongs on the auto-dispatch loop?
opts: Free local-agent work (research, drafts); paid + judgment stays manual
quiz.ex: That's your own designed split (ROLE.md): free/local auto-runs, paid + human-in-loop stays manual relay. The design was right — it was just never switched on.

mission.txt: With Claude (next local session): start agentwatch with a log, route one research task to a local agent, and watch one full add→claim→done cycle. This is the course's keystone mission.

levels.title: LEVEL 3 · THE GATE QUEUE — you are the slowest machine
levels.html:
The pattern the audit caught

DNS records. GoatCounter claims. The miklatgames repo. Ko-fi. The Gemini key. Every pipeline this month stalled at a Zorba-only gate — 5-minute actions that waited days because they arrived one at a time, mid-flow, when you were focused elsewhere.

The fix: batch the gates

  • One Gate Queue (a section in the nucleus, not scattered in handoffs).
  • One weekly 20-minute Keys Session — you clear ALL gates in a batch, dopamine-style, checklist down.
  • Claude pre-stages everything: exact URL, exact values, exact click path. You only execute.

Right now there are 3 in the queue: Ko-fi account, fresh Gemini key (new project), portal accounts.

quiz.q: Why did 5-minute gates stall pipelines for days?
opts: They arrived one-at-a-time mid-focus instead of batched into one session
quiz.ex: Single interrupts lose to whatever you're focused on — every time, by design of your brain. Batching turns 5 interrupts into one 20-minute checklist session you can actually win.

mission.txt: Do a Keys Session NOW: Ko-fi account → paste URL to Claude · Gemini key in a NEW project → paste · portal account emails. 20 minutes, three pipelines unblocked.

levels.title: LEVEL 4 · WIRE THE MONEY TOOLS + BOSS
levels.html:
Idle connections = blind spots

Stripe, Google Analytics, and Meta Ads MCPs are available and unwired. Lead capture and email automation are drafted, not live. Rule of thumb: a tool you can't see from the standup doesn't exist. Wire them in the order money flows: lead capture → booking → payment → analytics.

The nightly loop (your first real automation)

One recurring job: every night a local agent compiles a brief (competitor moves, your funnel numbers, open gates) and pushes it to the nucleus. You wake up to a board that already knows the state of the world. That's the 10pm task auto-close — the course's proof of success.

opts: A task auto-closes at 10pm while you sleep

boss.q: Stale tasks on the bus mainly destroy…
opts: Your trust in the board (and then the whole OS)
Batched into one weekly pre-staged Keys Session
Free local-agent work; paid + judgment stays manual

mission.txt: Commission the nightly brief: tell Claude 'set up the nightly research brief on agentwatch' in your next local session. When the first one lands in the nucleus overnight — course complete, for real.
